chapter_title.ar: باب أَنَّ مَنْ كَانَتْ لَهُ بَيِّنَةٌ فَلَا يَمِينَ عَلَيْهِ إِذَا أَقَامَهَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 14 – The one who has a proof for himself, so there is no swearing upon him when he establishes it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/284)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/284)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يُقِيمُ الْبَيِّنَةَ عَلَى حَقِّهِ هَلْ عَلَيْهِ أَنْ يُسْتَحْلَفَ قَالَ لَا .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from one of his companions, from Aasim Bin Humeyd, from Muhammad Bin Muslim who said,
‘I asked Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> about the man who establishes the proof upon his right, is there upon him that he should be swearing an oath?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from certain persons of his people from ‘Asem ibn Humayd from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has narrated the following: “I once asked abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who has testimony; if it is obligatory on him to take an oath. He (the Imam) said, ‘No, it is not obligatory on him/”
